preamble: The shift from the voiceless dental fricative [θ] to the voiceless labiodental fricative [f] is a form of th-fronting. Acoustically, these two sounds are very similar, leading to their merger in many dialects and in the speech of children during language acquisition.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
